---
title: "Data Prep & Cleaning"
author: "Melody Xiao"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

### 0. Setup

```{r load packages}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(sf)
library(tidycensus)
```

```{r tidycensus setup}
## DO NOT RUN if you already have a Census API key loaded
census_api_key("7e09c574348b580868c23ac0304cdea01e65413c", install = TRUE)
```

```{r making not in function}
'%!in%' <- Negate('%in%')
```

### 1. Loading data

Infectious disease incidence data were downloaded from NCDHHS (<https://epi.dph.ncdhhs.gov/cd/figures.html>). Data were downloaded one year at a time due to R import issues with multiple-year spreadsheets.

These data also have the adjusted population sizes of each county.

```{r NC infectious diseases}
id_2017 <- read.csv("C:/Users/mxiao/OneDrive - Duke University/Misc/CAFOs in NC/Data/Infectious diseases/NC_ID_2017.csv")

id_2018 <- read.csv("C:/Users/mxiao/OneDrive - Duke University/Misc/CAFOs in NC/Data/Infectious diseases/NC_ID_2018.csv")

id_2019 <- read.csv("C:/Users/mxiao/OneDrive - Duke University/Misc/CAFOs in NC/Data/Infectious diseases/NC_ID_2019.csv")

id_2020 <- read.csv("C:/Users/mxiao/OneDrive - Duke University/Misc/CAFOs in NC/Data/Infectious diseases/NC_ID_2020.csv")

id_2021 <- read.csv("C:/Users/mxiao/OneDrive - Duke University/Misc/CAFOs in NC/Data/Infectious diseases/NC_ID_2021.csv")

id_2022 <- read.csv("C:/Users/mxiao/OneDrive - Duke University/Misc/CAFOs in NC/Data/Infectious diseases/NC_ID_2022.csv")

id_2023 <- read.csv("C:/Users/mxiao/OneDrive - Duke University/Misc/CAFOs in NC/Data/Infectious diseases/NC_ID_2023.csv")
```

2017 and 2020 CAFO data come from personal correspondence with Dr. Christine Lawson; these spreadsheets were originally publicly available on the NC DEQ website but were replaced by the 2024 spreadsheet (<https://www.deq.nc.gov/about/divisions/water-resources/permitting/animal-feeding-operations/animal-facility-map>).

```{r CAFOs in NC}
cafo_2017 <- read.csv("C:/Users/mxiao/OneDrive - Duke University/Misc/CAFOs in NC/Data/CAFOs/CAFO 2017.csv")

cafo_2020 <- read.csv("C:/Users/mxiao/OneDrive - Duke University/Misc/CAFOs in NC/Data/CAFOs/CAFO 2020.csv")

cafo_2024 <- read.csv("C:/Users/mxiao/OneDrive - Duke University/Misc/CAFOs in NC/Data/CAFOs/CAFO 2024.csv")
```

Land area data for each county as of 1 July 2024 were obtained from the Office of State Budget and Management (<https://www.osbm.nc.gov/facts-figures/population-demographics/state-demographer/municipal-population-estimates/municipal-and-non-municipal-land-area>).

```{r land area}
land_area <- read.csv("C:/Users/mxiao/OneDrive - Duke University/Misc/CAFOs in NC/Data/State info/NC land area.csv")
```

Percent insured by county in North Carolina were obtained from ACS 5-year estimates for 2017-2023. This will be used to get percent uninsured estimates and serves as a proxy for healthcare access. We use 5-year estimates because not all of our counties are big enough for 1-year estimates.

```{r percent insured}
unins_2017 <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = c(perc_unins = "S2701_C05_001"),
  state = "NC",
  year = 2017
)

unins_2018 <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = c(perc_unins = "S2701_C05_001"),
  state = "NC",
  year = 2018
)

unins_2019 <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = c(perc_unins = "S2701_C05_001"),
  state = "NC",
  year = 2019
)

unins_2020 <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = c(perc_unins = "S2701_C05_001"),
  state = "NC",
  year = 2020
)

unins_2021 <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = c(perc_unins = "S2701_C05_001"),
  state = "NC",
  year = 2021
)

unins_2022 <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = c(perc_unins = "S2701_C05_001"),
  state = "NC",
  year = 2022
)

unins_2023 <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = c(perc_unins = "S2701_C05_001"),
  state = "NC",
  year = 2023
)
```

The poverty rate by county in North Carolina were obtained from ACS 5-year estimates for 2017-2023.

```{r poverty}
pov_2017 <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = c(perc_pov = "S1701_C03_001"),
  state = "NC",
  year = 2017
)

pov_2018 <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = c(perc_pov = "S1701_C03_001"),
  state = "NC",
  year = 2018
)

pov_2019 <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = c(perc_pov = "S1701_C03_001"),
  state = "NC",
  year = 2019
)

pov_2020 <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = c(perc_pov = "S1701_C03_001"),
  state = "NC",
  year = 2020
)

pov_2021 <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = c(perc_pov = "S1701_C03_001"),
  state = "NC",
  year = 2021
)

pov_2022 <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = c(perc_pov = "S1701_C03_001"),
  state = "NC",
  year = 2022
)

pov_2023 <- get_acs(
  geography = "county",
  variables = c(perc_pov = "S1701_C03_001"),
  state = "NC",
  year = 2023
)
```

The percent foreign-born by county in North Carolina were obtained from the North Carolina Office of State Budget and Management (long URL, linked [here](https://linc.osbm.nc.gov/explore/dataset/foreign-born-population-and-citizenship/analyze/?flg=en-us&disjunctive.name&disjunctive.imported_year&disjunctive.variable&%3D)). Foreign-borne is defined as naturalized residents and non-citizens.

```{r foreign born}
foreign_born <- read.csv("C:/Users/mxiao/OneDrive - Duke University/Misc/CAFOs in NC/Data/State info/foreign-born-population-and-citizenship.csv")
```

### 2. Diseases

We're only going to look at:

-   TB
-   other respiratory (grouped)

```{r diseases 2017}
## fix variable types
id_2017$County.Pop.Adjusted <- as.numeric(id_2017$County.Pop.Adjusted)
id_2017$County.Cases <- as.numeric(id_2017$County.Cases)
id_2017$County.Incidence <- as.numeric(id_2017$County.Incidence)

## set aside population sizes
pop_2017 <- id_2017[seq(1, nrow(id_2017), by=9),]
pop_2017 <- pop_2017 %>%
  select(County, County.Pop.Adjusted)

## specify TB and ORDs
id_2017$type[id_2017$Disease=="TB"] <- "TB"
id_2017$type <- ifelse(
    id_2017$Disease=="Pertussis" | id_2017$Disease=="Mumps" | 
    id_2017$Disease=="Meningococcal invasive disease" | 
    id_2017$Disease=="Legionellosis" |
    id_2017$Disease=="Haemophilus influenzae, invasive disease"|
    id_2017$Disease=="Influenza, adult death" | 
    id_2017$Disease=="Influenza, pediatric death" | 
    id_2017$Disease=="Influenza, NOVEL virus infection",
    "ORD", id_2017$type
)
id_2017 <- id_2017[!is.na(id_2017$type),]

## get pooled IRs for the ORDs
id_2017$n_case <- id_2017$County.Incidence*id_2017$County.Pop.Adjusted/100000

resp_2017 <- id_2017 %>%
  group_by(County, type) %>%
  summarize(cat_n = sum(n_case))
resp_2017 <- left_join(resp_2017, pop_2017, by = "County")

resp_2017$p_ir <- resp_2017$cat_n/resp_2017$County.Pop.Adjusted*100000
```

```{r diseases 2018}
## fix variable types
id_2018$County.Pop.Adjusted <- as.numeric(id_2018$County.Pop.Adjusted)
id_2018$County.Cases <- as.numeric(id_2018$County.Cases)
id_2018$County.Incidence <- as.numeric(id_2018$County.Incidence)

## specify TB and ORDs
id_2018$type[id_2018$Disease=="TB"] <- "TB"
id_2018$type <- ifelse(
    id_2018$Disease=="Pertussis" | id_2018$Disease=="Mumps" | 
    id_2018$Disease=="Meningococcal invasive disease" | 
    id_2018$Disease=="Legionellosis" |
    id_2018$Disease=="Haemophilus influenzae, invasive disease"|
    id_2018$Disease=="Influenza, adult death" | 
    id_2018$Disease=="Influenza, pediatric death" | 
    id_2018$Disease=="Influenza, NOVEL virus infection",
    "ORD", id_2018$type
)
id_2018 <- id_2018[!is.na(id_2018$type),]

## set aside population sizes
pop_2018 <- id_2018[seq(1, nrow(id_2018), by=9),]
pop_2018 <- pop_2018 %>%
  select(County, County.Pop.Adjusted)

## get pooled IRs for the ORDs
id_2018$n_case <- id_2018$County.Incidence*id_2018$County.Pop.Adjusted/100000

resp_2018 <- id_2018 %>%
  group_by(County, type) %>%
  summarize(cat_n = sum(n_case))
resp_2018 <- left_join(resp_2018, pop_2018, by = "County")

resp_2018$p_ir <- resp_2018$cat_n/resp_2018$County.Pop.Adjusted*100000
```

```{r diseases 2019}
## fix variable types
id_2019$County.Pop.Adjusted <- as.numeric(id_2019$County.Pop.Adjusted)
id_2019$County.Cases <- as.numeric(id_2019$County.Cases)
id_2019$County.Incidence <- as.numeric(id_2019$County.Incidence)

## specify TB and ORDs
id_2019$type[id_2019$Disease=="TB"] <- "TB"
id_2019$type <- ifelse(
    id_2019$Disease=="Pertussis" | id_2019$Disease=="Mumps" | 
    id_2019$Disease=="Meningococcal invasive disease" | 
    id_2019$Disease=="Legionellosis" |
    id_2019$Disease=="Haemophilus influenzae, invasive disease"|
    id_2019$Disease=="Influenza, adult death" | 
    id_2019$Disease=="Influenza, pediatric death" | 
    id_2019$Disease=="Influenza, NOVEL virus infection",
    "ORD", id_2019$type
)
id_2019 <- id_2019[!is.na(id_2019$type),]

## set aside population sizes
pop_2019 <- id_2019[seq(1, nrow(id_2019), by=9),]
pop_2019 <- pop_2019 %>%
  select(County, County.Pop.Adjusted)

## get pooled IRs for the ORDs
id_2019$n_case <- id_2019$County.Incidence*id_2019$County.Pop.Adjusted/100000

resp_2019 <- id_2019 %>%
  group_by(County, type) %>%
  summarize(cat_n = sum(n_case))
resp_2019 <- left_join(resp_2019, pop_2019, by = "County")

resp_2019$p_ir <- resp_2019$cat_n/resp_2019$County.Pop.Adjusted*100000
```

```{r diseases 2020}
## fix variable types
id_2020$County.Pop.Adjusted <- as.numeric(id_2020$County.Pop.Adjusted)
id_2020$County.Cases <- as.numeric(id_2020$County.Cases)
id_2020$County.Incidence <- as.numeric(id_2020$County.Incidence)

## specify TB and ORDs
id_2020$type[id_2020$Disease=="TB"] <- "TB"
id_2020$type <- ifelse(
    id_2020$Disease=="Pertussis" | id_2020$Disease=="Mumps" | 
    id_2020$Disease=="Meningococcal invasive disease" | 
    id_2020$Disease=="Legionellosis" |
    id_2020$Disease=="Haemophilus influenzae, invasive disease"|
    id_2020$Disease=="Influenza, adult death" | 
    id_2020$Disease=="Influenza, pediatric death" | 
    id_2020$Disease=="Influenza, NOVEL virus infection",
    "ORD", id_2020$type
)
id_2020 <- id_2020[!is.na(id_2020$type),]

## set aside population sizes
pop_2020 <- id_2020[seq(1, nrow(id_2020), by=9),]
pop_2020 <- pop_2020 %>%
  select(County, County.Pop.Adjusted)

## get pooled IRs for the ORDs
id_2020$n_case <- id_2020$County.Incidence*id_2020$County.Pop.Adjusted/100000

resp_2020 <- id_2020 %>%
  group_by(County, type) %>%
  summarize(cat_n = sum(n_case))
resp_2020 <- left_join(resp_2020, pop_2020, by = "County")

resp_2020$p_ir <- resp_2020$cat_n/resp_2020$County.Pop.Adjusted*100000
```

```{r diseases 2021}
## fix variable types
id_2021$County.Pop.Adjusted <- as.numeric(id_2021$County.Pop.Adjusted)
id_2021$County.Cases <- as.numeric(id_2021$County.Cases)
id_2021$County.Incidence <- as.numeric(id_2021$County.Incidence)

## specify TB and ORDs
id_2021$type[id_2021$Disease=="TB"] <- "TB"
id_2021$type <- ifelse(
    id_2021$Disease=="Pertussis" | id_2021$Disease=="Mumps" | 
    id_2021$Disease=="Meningococcal invasive disease" | 
    id_2021$Disease=="Legionellosis" |
    id_2021$Disease=="Haemophilus influenzae, invasive disease"|
    id_2021$Disease=="Influenza, adult death" | 
    id_2021$Disease=="Influenza, pediatric death" | 
    id_2021$Disease=="Influenza, NOVEL virus infection",
    "ORD", id_2021$type
)
id_2021 <- id_2021[!is.na(id_2021$type),]

## set aside population sizes
pop_2021 <- id_2021[seq(1, nrow(id_2021), by=9),]
pop_2021 <- pop_2021 %>%
  select(County, County.Pop.Adjusted)

## get pooled IRs for the ORDs
id_2021$n_case <- id_2021$County.Incidence*id_2021$County.Pop.Adjusted/100000

resp_2021 <- id_2021 %>%
  group_by(County, type) %>%
  summarize(cat_n = sum(n_case))
resp_2021 <- left_join(resp_2021, pop_2021, by = "County")

resp_2021$p_ir <- resp_2021$cat_n/resp_2021$County.Pop.Adjusted*100000
```

```{r diseases 2022}
## fix variable types
id_2022$County.Pop.Adjusted <- as.numeric(id_2022$County.Pop.Adjusted)
id_2022$County.Cases <- as.numeric(id_2022$County.Cases)
id_2022$County.Incidence <- as.numeric(id_2022$County.Incidence)

## specify TB and ORDs
id_2022$type[id_2022$Disease=="TB"] <- "TB"
id_2022$type <- ifelse(
    id_2022$Disease=="Pertussis" | id_2022$Disease=="Mumps" | 
    id_2022$Disease=="Meningococcal invasive disease" | 
    id_2022$Disease=="Legionellosis" |
    id_2022$Disease=="Haemophilus influenzae, invasive disease"|
    id_2022$Disease=="Influenza, adult death" | 
    id_2022$Disease=="Influenza, pediatric death" | 
    id_2022$Disease=="Influenza, NOVEL virus infection",
    "ORD", id_2022$type
)
id_2022 <- id_2022[!is.na(id_2022$type),]

## set aside population sizes
pop_2022 <- id_2022[seq(1, nrow(id_2022), by=9),]
pop_2022 <- pop_2022 %>%
  select(County, County.Pop.Adjusted)

## get pooled IRs for the ORDs
id_2022$n_case <- id_2022$County.Incidence*id_2022$County.Pop.Adjusted/100000

resp_2022 <- id_2022 %>%
  group_by(County, type) %>%
  summarize(cat_n = sum(n_case))
resp_2022 <- left_join(resp_2022, pop_2022, by = "County")

resp_2022$p_ir <- resp_2022$cat_n/resp_2022$County.Pop.Adjusted*100000
```

```{r diseases 2023}
## fix variable types
id_2023$County.Pop.Adjusted <- as.numeric(id_2023$County.Pop.Adjusted)
id_2023$County.Cases <- as.numeric(id_2023$County.Cases)
id_2023$County.Incidence <- as.numeric(id_2023$County.Incidence)

## specify TB and ORDs
id_2023$type[id_2023$Disease=="TB"] <- "TB"
id_2023$type <- ifelse(
    id_2023$Disease=="Pertussis" | id_2023$Disease=="Mumps" | 
    id_2023$Disease=="Meningococcal invasive disease" | 
    id_2023$Disease=="Legionellosis" |
    id_2023$Disease=="Haemophilus influenzae, invasive disease"|
    id_2023$Disease=="Influenza, adult death" | 
    id_2023$Disease=="Influenza, pediatric death" | 
    id_2023$Disease=="Influenza, NOVEL virus infection",
    "ORD", id_2023$type
)
id_2023 <- id_2023[!is.na(id_2023$type),]

## set aside population sizes
pop_2023 <- id_2023[seq(1, nrow(id_2023), by=9),]
pop_2023 <- pop_2023 %>%
  select(County, County.Pop.Adjusted)

## get pooled IRs for the ORDs
id_2023$n_case <- id_2023$County.Incidence*id_2023$County.Pop.Adjusted/100000

resp_2023 <- id_2023 %>%
  group_by(County, type) %>%
  summarize(cat_n = sum(n_case))
resp_2023 <- left_join(resp_2023, pop_2023, by = "County")

resp_2023$p_ir <- resp_2023$cat_n/resp_2023$County.Pop.Adjusted*100000
```

Put it all together into one dataframe; should have 1400 rows (100 counties, 2 disease categories, 7 years).

```{r putting together}
resp_df <- rbind(resp_2017, resp_2018, resp_2019, resp_2020, resp_2021, resp_2022, resp_2023)

years <- c(rep(2017, times = 200), rep(2018, times = 200), rep(2019, times = 200), rep(2020, times = 200), rep(2021, times = 200), rep(2022, times = 200), rep(2023, times = 200))
resp_df$year <- years
saveRDS(resp_df, "resp_df.RDS")
```

### 3. Swine CAFOs

We have three spreadsheets, each formatted a little differently (2017, 2020, 2024). What we want to pull is how many CAFOs are active by county in each of the years between 2017 and 2023.

```{r clean}
## isolate active swine CAFOs
cafo_2017 <- cafo_2017[cafo_2017$Permit.Status=="Active",]

cafo_2017 <- cafo_2017[cafo_2017$Regulated.Operation=="Swine",]
cafo_2020 <- cafo_2020[cafo_2020$Regulated.Operation=="Swine",]
cafo_2024 <- cafo_2024[cafo_2024$Regulated.Operation=="Swine",]

## select relevant columns
cafo_2017 <- cafo_2017 %>%
  select(Permit.Number, Facility.Name, Original.Issued.Date, Expiration.Date, County.Name, Latitude, Longitude) %>%
  rename(Issued.Date = Original.Issued.Date) %>%
  distinct(Permit.Number, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    sheet = 2017,
    start.year = str_sub(Issued.Date, -4, -1),
    end.year = str_sub(Expiration.Date, -4, -1)
  )
cafo_2017$start.year <- as.numeric(cafo_2017$start.year)
cafo_2017$end.year <- as.numeric(cafo_2017$end.year)
  
cafo_2020 <- cafo_2020 %>%
  select(Permit.Number, Facility.Name, Issued.Date, Expiration.Date, County.Name, Latitude, Longitude) %>%
  distinct(Permit.Number, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    sheet = 2020,
    start.year = str_sub(Issued.Date, -4, -1),
    end.year = str_sub(Expiration.Date, -4, -1)
  )
cafo_2020$start.year <- as.numeric(cafo_2020$start.year)
cafo_2020$end.year <- as.numeric(cafo_2020$end.year)

cafo_2024 <- cafo_2024 %>%
  select(Permit.Number, Facility.Name, Issued.Date, Expiration.Date, County.Name, Location.Lat.Num, Location.Long.Num) %>%
  distinct(Permit.Number, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  rename(Latitude = Location.Lat.Num, Longitude = Location.Long.Num) %>%
  mutate(
    sheet = 2024,
    start.year = str_sub(Issued.Date, -4, -1),
    end.year = str_sub(Expiration.Date, -4, -1)
  )
cafo_2024$start.year <- as.numeric(cafo_2024$start.year)
cafo_2024$end.year <- as.numeric(cafo_2024$end.year)

## join
cafo_df <- rbind(cafo_2017, cafo_2020, cafo_2024)
saveRDS(cafo_df, "cafo_df.RDS")
```

```{r CAFOs by year}
years <- 2017:2023
cafo_df <- drop_na(cafo_df)

## was the CAFO active during a given year?
cafo_df$a2017 <- NA
cafo_df$a2018 <- NA
cafo_df$a2019 <- NA
cafo_df$a2020 <- NA
cafo_df$a2021 <- NA
cafo_df$a2022 <- NA
cafo_df$a2023 <- NA

for(i in 1:nrow(cafo_df)){
  if(2017 %in% cafo_df$start.year[i]:cafo_df$end.year[i]){
    cafo_df$a2017[i] <- TRUE
  } else {
    cafo_df$a2017[i] <- FALSE
  }
  
  if(2018 %in% cafo_df$start.year[i]:cafo_df$end.year[i]){
    cafo_df$a2018[i] <- TRUE
  } else {
    cafo_df$a2018[i] <- FALSE
  }
  
  if(2019 %in% cafo_df$start.year[i]:cafo_df$end.year[i]){
    cafo_df$a2019[i] <- TRUE
  } else {
    cafo_df$a2019[i] <- FALSE
  }
  
  if(2020 %in% cafo_df$start.year[i]:cafo_df$end.year[i]){
    cafo_df$a2020[i] <- TRUE
  } else {
    cafo_df$a2020[i] <- FALSE
  }
  
  if(2021 %in% cafo_df$start.year[i]:cafo_df$end.year[i]){
    cafo_df$a2021[i] <- TRUE
  } else {
    cafo_df$a2021[i] <- FALSE
  }
  
  if(2022 %in% cafo_df$start.year[i]:cafo_df$end.year[i]){
    cafo_df$a2022[i] <- TRUE
  } else {
    cafo_df$a2022[i] <- FALSE
  }
  
  if(2023 %in% cafo_df$start.year[i]:cafo_df$end.year[i]){
    cafo_df$a2023[i] <- TRUE
  } else {
    cafo_df$a2023[i] <- FALSE
  }
}

## separate by year
clist_2017 <- cafo_df[cafo_df$a2017==TRUE,]
clist_2017 <- clist_2017 %>%
  distinct(Permit.Number, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  group_by(County.Name) %>%
  summarize(count = n()) %>%
  mutate(year = 2017)

clist_2018 <- cafo_df[cafo_df$a2018==TRUE,]
clist_2018 <- clist_2018 %>%
  distinct(Permit.Number, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  group_by(County.Name) %>%
  summarize(count = n())%>%
  mutate(year = 2018)

clist_2019 <- cafo_df[cafo_df$a2019==TRUE,]
clist_2019 <- clist_2019 %>%
  distinct(Permit.Number, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  group_by(County.Name) %>%
  summarize(count = n()) %>%
  mutate(year = 2019)

clist_2020 <- cafo_df[cafo_df$a2020==TRUE,]
clist_2020 <- clist_2020 %>%
  distinct(Permit.Number, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  group_by(County.Name) %>%
  summarize(count = n()) %>%
  mutate(year = 2020)

clist_2021 <- cafo_df[cafo_df$a2021==TRUE,]
clist_2021 <- clist_2021 %>%
  distinct(Permit.Number, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  group_by(County.Name) %>%
  summarize(count = n()) %>%
  mutate(year = 2021)

clist_2022 <- cafo_df[cafo_df$a2022==TRUE,]
clist_2022 <- clist_2022 %>%
  distinct(Permit.Number, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  group_by(County.Name) %>%
  summarize(count = n()) %>%
  mutate(year = 2022)

clist_2023 <- cafo_df[cafo_df$a2023==TRUE,]
clist_2023 <- clist_2023 %>%
  distinct(Permit.Number, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  group_by(County.Name) %>%
  summarize(count = n()) %>%
  mutate(year = 2023)

## combine
clist_df <- rbind(clist_2017, clist_2018, clist_2019, clist_2020, clist_2021, clist_2022, clist_2023)
```

Lastly, we want to get the density of CAFOs per square mile by county.

```{r CAFO density}
clist_df <- clist_df %>%
  rename(County = County.Name)
clist_df <- left_join(clist_df, land_area, by = "County")

clist_df$cafo_psm <- clist_df$count/clist_df$Total.Land.Area
```

Lastly, drop the "Out of State" entries and save.

```{r finalize}
clist_df <- drop_na(clist_df)

saveRDS(clist_df, "clist_df.RDS")
```

### 4. Healthcare access/Uninsured

We want to combine all the data into one dataframe, with uninsured rate by county by year.

```{r uninsured}
unins_2017 <- unins_2017 %>%
  select(NAME, estimate) %>%
  rename(
    County = NAME,
    perc_unins = estimate
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    County = str_remove(County, " County, North Carolina"),
    year = 2017
  )

unins_2018 <- unins_2018 %>%
  select(NAME, estimate) %>%
  rename(
    County = NAME,
    perc_unins = estimate
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    County = str_remove(County, " County, North Carolina"),
    year = 2018
  )

unins_2019 <- unins_2019 %>%
  select(NAME, estimate) %>%
  rename(
    County = NAME,
    perc_unins = estimate
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    County = str_remove(County, " County, North Carolina"),
    year = 2019
  )

unins_2020 <- unins_2020 %>%
  select(NAME, estimate) %>%
  rename(
    County = NAME,
    perc_unins = estimate
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    County = str_remove(County, " County, North Carolina"),
    year = 2020
  )

unins_2021 <- unins_2021 %>%
  select(NAME, estimate) %>%
  rename(
    County = NAME,
    perc_unins = estimate
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    County = str_remove(County, " County, North Carolina"),
    year = 2021
  )

unins_2022 <- unins_2022 %>%
  select(NAME, estimate) %>%
  rename(
    County = NAME,
    perc_unins = estimate
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    County = str_remove(County, " County, North Carolina"),
    year = 2022
  )

unins_2023 <- unins_2023 %>%
  select(NAME, estimate) %>%
  rename(
    County = NAME,
    perc_unins = estimate
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    County = str_remove(County, " County, North Carolina"),
    year = 2023
  )

## combine
unins_df <- rbind(unins_2017, unins_2018, unins_2019, unins_2020, unins_2021, unins_2022, unins_2023)
saveRDS(unins_df, "unins_df.RDS")
```

### 5. Poverty

We will add the percent of county living in poverty.

```{r poverty}
pov_2017 <- pov_2017 %>%
  select(NAME, estimate) %>%
  rename(
    County = NAME,
    perc_pov = estimate
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    County = str_remove(County, " County, North Carolina"),
    year = 2017
  )

pov_2018 <- pov_2018 %>%
  select(NAME, estimate) %>%
  rename(
    County = NAME,
    perc_pov = estimate
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    County = str_remove(County, " County, North Carolina"),
    year = 2018
  )

pov_2019 <- pov_2019 %>%
  select(NAME, estimate) %>%
  rename(
    County = NAME,
    perc_pov = estimate
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    County = str_remove(County, " County, North Carolina"),
    year = 2019
  )

pov_2020 <- pov_2020 %>%
  select(NAME, estimate) %>%
  rename(
    County = NAME,
    perc_pov = estimate
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    County = str_remove(County, " County, North Carolina"),
    year = 2020
  )

pov_2021 <- pov_2021 %>%
  select(NAME, estimate) %>%
  rename(
    County = NAME,
    perc_pov = estimate
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    County = str_remove(County, " County, North Carolina"),
    year = 2021
  )

pov_2022 <- pov_2022 %>%
  select(NAME, estimate) %>%
  rename(
    County = NAME,
    perc_pov = estimate
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    County = str_remove(County, " County, North Carolina"),
    year = 2022
  )

pov_2023 <- pov_2023 %>%
  select(NAME, estimate) %>%
  rename(
    County = NAME,
    perc_pov = estimate
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    County = str_remove(County, " County, North Carolina"),
    year = 2023
  )

## combine
pov_df <- rbind(pov_2017, pov_2018, pov_2019, pov_2020, pov_2021, pov_2022, pov_2023)
saveRDS(pov_df, "pov_df.RDS")
```

### 6. Foreign-born

Already in one dataframe, just need to clean it up a little. We will calculate the percent later.

```{r foreign-born}
foreign_df <- foreign_born %>%
  select(Area.Name, Year, Value) %>%
  rename(
    County = Area.Name,
    year = Year,
    n_foreign = Value
  ) %>%
  mutate(County = str_remove(County, " County"))
```

### 7. Combine and save

Combine all the data into one dataframe in panel format for longitudinal analyses.

```{r combine}
df <- left_join(resp_df, clist_df, by = c("County", "year"))
df <- left_join(df, unins_df, by = c("County", "year"))
df <- left_join(df, pov_df, by = c("County", "year"))
df <- left_join(df, foreign_df, by = c("County", "year"))

df <- df %>%
  select(-Total.Land.Area)
df <- left_join(df, land_area, by = "County")

df$count <- ifelse(is.na(df$count), 0, df$count)
df$cafo_psm <- ifelse(is.na(df$cafo_psm), 0, df$cafo_psm)
```

We still need to get population density and *percent* foreign-born, not just count.

```{r more variables}
## population density
df$pop_dens <- df$County.Pop.Adjusted/df$Total.Land.Area

## percent foreign-born
df$perc_foreign <- df$n_foreign/df$County.Pop.Adjusted
```

Rearrange to make more sense.

```{r rearrange}
df <- df %>%
  select(year, County, type, cat_n, County.Pop.Adjusted, p_ir, count, cafo_psm, perc_unins, perc_pov, n_foreign, Total.Land.Area, pop_dens, perc_foreign)
```

Save final df!

```{r saving dfs}
saveRDS(df, "df.RDS")
```
